steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: Lines l and k are parallel, and transversal t crosses both. Angles x and y are co-interior angles (same-side interior / consecutive interior angles) — they sit between the parallel lines on the same side of the transversal. The Co-Interior Angles Theorem states these always sum to $180^{\circ}$: $$x + y = 180^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Apply the given constraint x > 100
steps.content: From $$x + y = 180^{\circ}$$ we get $$y = 180^{\circ} - x.$$ Since $x \gt 100^{\circ}$: $$y = 180^{\circ} - x \lt 180^{\circ} - 100^{\circ} = 80^{\circ}$$ So $y \lt 80^{\circ}$ must always be true.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y \lt 80$: As derived above, $x = 180 - x$ and $x \gt 100^{\circ}$, so $y \lt 80^{\circ}$. This always holds. ✓
  • Choice B — $y \gt 80$: This would require y > 80, but we just showed y < 80 when x > 100. ✗
  • Choice C — $x + y \lt 180$: Co-interior angles always sum to exactly $180^{\circ}$, not less than $180^{\circ}$. ✗
  • Choice D — $x + y \gt 180$: Co-interior angles always sum to exactly $180^{\circ}$, not greater than $180^{\circ}$. ✗
